_schema.description: Matrix dataset. rows[] and cols[] define the two categorical axes. values[][] is a 2D array indexed [row][col] in the same order as rows[] and cols[]. All values are on a 0–100 scale.

fields.title: string — chart headline
fields.unit: string — what each cell value represents
fields.colorDomain: [number, number] — min and max of the color scale (usually [0, 100] or [dataMin, dataMax])
fields.rows[]: string — row category labels (displayed on left axis)
fields.cols[]: string — column category labels (displayed on top axis)
fields.values[][]: number — 2D array, values[i][j] is the cell at row i, column j

_schema.replace: Swap rows[], cols[], and values[][] with your categories and data. Ensure values[][] has exactly rows.length arrays, each of length cols.length. Adjust colorDomain if your scale is not 0–100.

title: AI Adoption Intensity Index by Region and Sector
